title: Tanarius maingayi
intro: Tanarius maingayi, also known as Tanacetum maingayi, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic properties, providing relief from pain and reducing the risk of infections. The bioactive constituents of Tanarius maingayi include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems.

The classification of Tanarius maingayi is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the division Magnoliophyta.
